title: Google releases Gemma 4 QAT models for efficient on-device AI
summary: Google has released Gemma 4 QAT models, optimized via quantization-aware training for efficient inference on mobile devices and laptops. This enables large language models to run efficiently on edge devices, reducing reliance on cloud servers and improving privacy and accessibility. The models support multimodal inputs (text, image, audio) and are available in 3.2GB versions for local execution. Community analysis shows Unsloth's quantized variants may outperform Google's official QAT versions in some metrics.
context: Quantization-aware training (QAT) simulates quantization effects during training, yielding better accuracy than post-training quantization (PTQ). Gemma 4 is a family of open models from Google DeepMind focused on reasoning and agentic workflows. This release includes QAT variants specifically optimized for mobile and laptop deployment.
discussion: Users demonstrated running the model locally on a Mac with a 3.2GB download. Some noted Unsloth's quantizations achieve near-100% accuracy compared to BF16 and outperform Google's official QAT. Others praised the rapid progress of the Gemma ecosystem and speculated about partnerships with Apple.
